danger_signals.visual_form: Printed poster shown before each stage depicting the target outlaw
danger_signals.meaning: Identifies the stage boss threat and establishes danger narrative

danger_signals.visual_form: Outlaws, bandits, and convicts distributed throughout levels
danger_signals.meaning: Immediate combat threat to player; environmental population of hostile forces
High-damage threat requiring reactive gameplay (must be shot/thrown back)

danger_signals.visual_form: Hanging rocks, barrels, torches positioned above walkways
danger_signals.meaning: Environmental hazards that cause damage on contact; spatial danger

danger_signals.visual_form: Visible flames and blast effects in environment
danger_signals.meaning: Instant-kill hazard; world-state danger manifested visually

danger_signals.visual_form: Fixed gun emplacements in final stage
danger_signals.meaning: Boss arena danger; high-volume fire threat

danger_signals.visual_form: Livestock entities moving through stage space
danger_signals.meaning: Environmental collision hazard causing life loss

safety_signals.meaning: Safe zones for power-up acquisition; temporary respite from open-stage threats

safety_signals.visual_form: Gold and silver sheriff's badges, extra weapons
safety_signals.meaning: Capacity increase signals (automatic fire, dual weapons); protective advantage

safety_signals.visual_form: Life counter display; multiple lives available before game over
safety_signals.meaning: Persistent survival state; players can absorb hits

safety_signals.visual_form: Continue prompt after life loss; limited-time credit insertion window
safety_signals.meaning: Temporary reprieve from failure; game state restoration available

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Sheriff's badge (gold/silver variants used as power-ups)
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Protagonist positioned at level start; visual distinction through character design (Steve, Billy Cool, Bob, Cormano Wild)

faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Boss characters visually distinct from common enemies; hierarchy of threat
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Saloon environments; frontier locations; eight named boss encounters (Simon Greedwell, Paco Loco, Chief Scalpem, Sir Richard Rose, etc.)

faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Female bandits (carry dynamite); sack-carrying bandits (drop power-ups); standard gunmen
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Distributed throughout stage terrain; variable density by stage

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: After all eight stages cleared, game restarts with increased difficulty; enemy density/behavior intensifies
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Enemy spawn rates increase; boss patterns become more aggressive; damage scaling

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Saloon environments; horseback riding sequences; train stage; frontier architecture; period-appropriate weaponry
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Thematic enemy types (outlaws, sheriff pursuits); stage-specific mechanics (bulls, mounted combat)

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Wanted posters preceding each boss; bonus point system for defeating high-value targets
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Boss encounters are mandatory progression gates; damage dealer receives full stage bonus in multiplayer

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Each bounty hunter wields distinct weapon (Steve/Billy: revolvers; Bob: rifle; Cormano: double-barrel shotgun); Cormano catches sombrero after stage 5 boss (visual cosmetic state change)
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Weapon firing patterns differ by character; shotgun provides close-range advantage; rifle extends effective range

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Power-up collection (gold badge for automatic fire; silver for dual weapons); both can be equipped simultaneously
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Fire rate and damage output increase; player becomes more capable threat; visual weapon behavior changes

environmental_storytelling.example: Each stage begins with visual presentation of the target outlaw's wanted poster, establishing narrative justification for boss encounter and communicating threat level through poster iconography
Progression through diverse Old West locations (saloons, open range, train, horseback terrain, final stage with Gatling guns) communicates geographic expansion and escalating danger of frontier
Cormano wearing captured sombrero after stage 5 boss defeat serves as persistent visual trophy indicating player achievement and character personality

Stage-specific enemy populations (female dynamite carriers, sack-carrying power-up bandits, standard gunmen, boss variants) communicate spatial threats and resource availability through visual presence
Post-stage 2 and 5 bonus stages present shooting galleries with outlaws; Genesis version substitutes horseback wagon chase with coin toss, reinforcing thematic progression

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Displayed in wanted poster; visually distinct from common enemies; positioned as final-stage threat
Follow scripted attack patterns; represent stage-completion gate; multiplayer damage dealer receives full bonus
Carry visible dynamite; must be shot to neutralize or thrown back to redirect; represent reactive threat requiring different strategy than standard gunmen
Drop power-up items upon defeat; encourage targeted elimination for resource acquisition
Visual state change: catches and dons captured sombrero; cosmetic modification persists for remaining gameplay; communicates character personality and player achievement
Four distinct visual designs (Steve, Billy Cool, Bob, Cormano Wild); each wields thematically appropriate weapon; 2-player mode allows character choice; 4-player assigns unique character per player
Distributed population indicating persistent threat; respond to player proximity; variable density by stage and world-state (difficulty)

notes: Sunset Riders communicates world-state primarily through location theming (Old West progression), character cosmetics (Cormano's sombrero), and enemy type distribution. Danger is signaled through wanted posters (narrative framing), visible hazards (fire, dynamite, overhead obstacles), and environmental populations of distinct enemy types. The bounty hunter faction is represented through character selection and sheriff's badge iconography. Faction conflict is implicit rather than visually complex: player bounty hunters vs. named outlaw bosses + generic criminal populations. No complex political systems detected; world-state changes are linear progression-based (difficulty scaling, location advancement). SNES version maintains faithful arcade port with minimal visual changes to core communication systems.
